rules.name: allow-fs-read
rules.description: Allow reading files anywhere (recon's core function).
rules.principle: The analyst may read filesystem contents to perform recon.
rules.reason: Reading is low-risk in a network-isolated sandbox; it is the analyst's core function.

rules.name: allow-git-read
rules.principle: The analyst may read repository history to triage vulnerabilities.
rules.reason: Git history reads are read-only and central to vulnerability triage.

rules.name: allow-git-branch-name
rules.description: Allow git operations that reference a branch name (a benign identifier).
rules.principle: Naming a branch is not itself a privileged action.
rules.reason: A branch name is a benign identifier; risk lives in network/history roles, gated separately.

rules.name: allow-git-commit-message
rules.description: Allow git operations that carry a commit message (benign content).
rules.principle: A commit message is benign text content.
rules.reason: A commit message is benign; the history-write itself is gated by the write-history role.

rules.name: allow-web-search
rules.description: Allow web_search (no URL is fetched; results are search hits).
rules.principle: The analyst may search the web for vulnerability information.
rules.reason: web_search returns search results without fetching attacker-controlled page content.

rules.name: allow-fetch-recon-domains
rules.description: Allow http_fetch to the curated security-recon domain allowlist.
rules.principle: The analyst may fetch from trusted security research sources.
rules.reason: Curated NVD/CVE/MITRE/OSV/CISA/exploit-db/OWASP/registry sources are trusted recon inputs.

rules.name: escalate-fetch-other-urls
rules.description: Escalate http_fetch to any domain not on the recon allowlist.
rules.principle: Human oversight: fetching off-allowlist domains is the exfiltration boundary.
rules.reason: An off-allowlist fetch is the indirect-prompt-injection / exfiltration boundary — a human must approve the destination.

rules.name: escalate-fs-write
rules.description: Escalate filesystem writes outside the sandbox workspace.
rules.principle: Human oversight: writing outside the workspace leaves the sandbox's reach.
rules.reason: Writes inside the workspace are structurally allowed; writes outside it require human approval.

rules.name: escalate-fs-delete
rules.description: Escalate filesystem deletes/moves outside the sandbox workspace.
rules.principle: Human oversight: deleting outside the workspace is destructive.
rules.reason: Deletes inside the workspace are structurally allowed; deletes outside it require human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-write-path
rules.description: Escalate git operations that write paths outside the sandbox workspace.
rules.principle: Human oversight: git writes outside the workspace leave the sandbox's reach.
rules.reason: Git write-paths inside the workspace are structurally allowed; outside it requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-delete-path
rules.description: Escalate git operations that delete paths outside the sandbox workspace.
rules.principle: Human oversight: git deletes outside the workspace are destructive.
rules.reason: Git delete-paths inside the workspace are structurally allowed; outside it requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-write-history
rules.description: Escalate git history mutations (commit/merge/rebase/reset/...) outside the workspace.
rules.principle: Human oversight: rewriting history outside the workspace is consequential.
rules.reason: History writes inside the workspace are structurally allowed; outside it requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-delete-history
rules.description: Escalate git history deletions (branch -D, stash drop, tag -d, ...) outside the workspace.
rules.principle: Human oversight: deleting history outside the workspace is destructive.
rules.reason: History deletes inside the workspace are structurally allowed; outside it requires human approval.

rules.name: escalate-git-remote-url
rules.description: Escalate every git network operation (fetch/pull/push/clone) and remote config.
rules.principle: Human oversight: git network egress and remote redirection require approval.
rules.reason: Git network ops egress data and git_remote rewrites redirect future egress — a human must approve.

listDefinitions.name: security-recon-domains
listDefinitions.principle: The analyst may fetch web content from curated security research sources.
listDefinitions.generationPrompt: Hand-authored: NVD/CVE/MITRE/CWE/CAPEC/ATT&CK, OSV, CISA, exploit-db, OWASP, FIRST/CVSS, vendor advisory trackers (Red Hat/Debian/Ubuntu/Rapid7/Tenable/Snyk/VulDB), Openwall/Packetstorm/SecLists, and package registries (PyPI/npm/RubyGems/crates.io/Go/Maven/Packagist/NuGet). See generated/dynamic-lists.json for resolved values.
